---
title: "Client Report - Famous Names"
subtitle: "Unit 1 Task 4"
author: "Camila Rojas"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
        source: false
        toggle: true
        caption: See code
execute: 
  warning: false
    
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *

LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)
```

```{python}
# Learn morea about Code Cells: https://quarto.org/docs/reference/cells/cells-jupyter.html

# Include and execute your code here
df = pd.read_csv("https://github.com/byuidatascience/data4names/raw/master/data-raw/names_year/names_year.csv")
```

## STRETCH TASK

__Reproduce the chart Elliot using the data from the names_year.csv file. If, after a couple of hours, you can get the chart close, but not exactly the same, that is okay. You will learn more to the degree you push yourself.__ 

```{python}

elliot = df.query("name == 'Elliot' and 1950 <=year")

elliot_plot = (
  ggplot(elliot, aes(x="year",y="Total",color="name"))+
  geom_line()+
  scale_color_manual(values={'Elliot':'blue'})+
  scale_x_continuous(format="d", limits=[1950,2025])+
  labs(
    title="Elliot.... What?",
    x="Year")+
  geom_vline(xintercept=1982, linetype=5,color="red")+
  geom_text(x=1974, y=1300, label="E.T. released", color="black", size=6)+
  geom_vline(xintercept=1985, linetype=5,color="red")+
  geom_text(x=1994, y=1300, label="Second release", color="black", size=6)+
  geom_vline(xintercept=2002, linetype=5,color="red")+
  geom_text(x=2010, y=1300, label="Third release", color="black", size=6)+
  theme(
    panel_grid=element_line(color="white"),
    panel_background=element_rect(fill="#e7ebf5", size=0),
    line=element_blank()
  )
)
elliot_plot
```
